current_description: Circle the bag clockwise with the foot before a toe stall.
fb_org_description: Circle the footbag in between toe delays. Either in to out or out to in (both worth the same adds).
fb_org_useful_points: circle the bag between two toe delays; either direction, equal ADD
proposed_about_text: A single dexterity that circles the foot completely around the bag between two toe delays. It can go inside-to-outside or outside-to-inside; both carry the same value.
proposed_movement_intuition: Keep the bag floating at toe height and pass the foot fully around it in one smooth loop, recatching on the same toe.

fb_org_description: Delay on the inside surface of the foot with the catching leg tucked behind the support leg. Bend the support leg deeply and lean over it for balance.
fb_org_useful_points: inside-surface contact; catching leg tucked behind support leg (cross-body); deep support-leg bend; lean over support leg
proposed_about_text: A delay caught on the inside surface of the foot with the catching leg tucked behind the support leg, cross-body. It is a foundational stall surface underpinning a large cross-body family; balance comes from the support leg.
proposed_movement_intuition: Tuck the catching leg behind the support leg so the inside of the foot meets the bag across your body; bend the support leg deeply and lean over it as the bag settles.

current_description: opposite of pixie direction; acts as +1 set modifier
fb_org_description: From a toe delay circle the bag out to in and catch on the toe of the support leg.
fb_org_useful_points: from a toe delay; circle out-to-in; catch on support-leg toe
proposed_about_text: A dexterity that circles the foot around the bag outside-to-inside, recaught on the toe of the support leg. It is the directional mirror of the pixie.
proposed_movement_intuition: From a toe delay, swing the foot around the bag outside-to-inside and bring it onto the toe of the supporting leg.

current_description: leg passes under bag opposite direction; acts as +1 set modifier
fb_org_description: From a toe delay circle the bag in to out and catch on the toe of the support leg.
fb_org_useful_points: from a toe delay; circle in-to-out; catch on support-leg toe
proposed_about_text: A dexterity that circles the foot around the bag inside-to-outside, recaught on the toe of the support leg. It is the directional mirror of the fairy.
proposed_movement_intuition: From a toe delay, sweep the foot around the bag inside-to-outside and bring it onto the toe of the supporting leg.

fb_org_description: Pass the support leg over the bag and catch on a delay (e.g. from a left toe stall, hop off the right leg over the bag to a right toe delay).
fb_org_useful_points: support leg passes over the top of the bag; hop to clear it; recatch on a toe delay
proposed_about_text: A foundational dexterity in which the support leg passes up and over the top of the bag before a delay recatch. It is the root of a broad leg-over family.
proposed_movement_intuition: From a toe delay, hop off the support leg and lift it up and over the top of the bag, then bring the bag onto a toe delay with the setting foot.

current_description: Toe-set dex outside the supporting leg, recaught on the opposite toe (in-to-out dex).
fb_org_description: Set straight up and swing the support leg in to out over the bag, catching on a toe delay. From a toe-set the support leg first swings under then back over.
fb_org_useful_points: set straight up; support leg swings in-to-out; from a toe set it passes under then back over; clipper-set easier
proposed_about_text: A toe-set dexterity in which the support leg swings over the bag inside-to-outside and the bag is recaught on the opposite toe. It is the reverse of the illusion, settable from a toe or (easier) a clipper delay.
proposed_movement_intuition: Set straight up and swing the support leg inside-to-outside over the bag; from a toe set, the support leg first passes under the bag then back over it.

fb_org_description: Spin into a clipper delay. Set straight in front waist high, turn so the bag passes behind your back, keep eyes on the bag, then turn quickly and catch on the inside surface (clipper).
fb_org_useful_points: it is a SPIN into a clipper; set waist-high in front; bag passes behind the back; eyes on bag; quick recatch cross-body
proposed_about_text: A body-spin that finishes in a clipper delay: the set goes straight up in front, and a turn carries the bag behind the back before a cross-body recatch on the inside of the foot. Anchors the osis family.
proposed_movement_intuition: Set waist-high and straight in front, turn so the bag passes behind your back keeping your eyes on it, then turn quickly to recatch on the inside surface in a clipper delay.

current_description: Rotational body-spin dex; anchor of the whirl family.
fb_org_description: Set up, then with the support leg circle the bag from the front up and over. Hop off the setting leg and catch on a clipper. Point toes down to go faster and avoid snagging.
fb_org_useful_points: support leg circles bag front-up-over (NOT a body spin); hop off setting leg; clipper catch; toes-down tip
proposed_about_text: A dexterity in which the support leg circles the bag from the front, up and over the top, before a clipper recatch. Anchors the whirl family; led by the support leg, not a body spin.
proposed_movement_intuition: Circle the support leg around the bag from the front, up and over, then hop off the setting leg and catch on a clipper; point the toes down through the circle to move faster and avoid snagging.

current_description: Reverse-direction rotational dex; anchor of the swirl family.
fb_org_description: Cross-body inside around-the-world; the BACK dexterity brings the foot around the bag from behind then up and over the top.
fb_org_useful_points: reverse-direction cross-body around-the-world; foot comes around from behind then up and over
proposed_about_text: A cross-body dexterity that circles the bag in the reverse direction of a standard around-the-world, on the clipper surface; the foot travels around the bag from behind, then up and over.
proposed_movement_intuition: From a clipper, circle the foot around the bag starting from behind it, then up and over the top to recatch cross-body.

fb_org_description: A miraging clipper delay; plant the setting leg, bring the support leg over in-to-out in one motion close to the other leg, into a clipper on the other side. Turn slightly toward the dex.
fb_org_useful_points: mirage into a clipper (not toe); legs stay close; turn toward the dexterity to recover cross-body
proposed_about_text: A mirage motion that resolves into a clipper delay rather than a toe delay; the support leg crosses the bag inside-to-outside in one continuous motion and lands cross-body. Anchors the drifter cluster.
proposed_movement_intuition: From a clipper, plant the setting leg and bring the support leg over the bag inside-to-outside in one motion, keeping it close to the other leg; turn slightly toward the dexterity to land cross-body.

fb_org_description: An atomic-set leg-over; the support leg first circles out-to-in to re-plant, then the original foot performs an out-to-in leg-over.
fb_org_useful_points: two dexterities off one set; first leg circles out-to-in to re-plant; original foot does an out-to-in leg-over
proposed_about_text: A leg-over preceded by an atomic set, so two dexterity motions share one set. The support leg first circles the bag outside-to-inside to re-establish support, then the original foot completes an outside-to-inside leg-over.
proposed_movement_intuition: Hop off the support leg and circle it around the bag outside-to-inside to re-plant, then bring the original foot over the bag outside-to-inside into the delay.

fb_org_description: From a clipper set, bring the setting leg around the bag out-to-in twice, hop off the support leg, recatch on a clipper with the support leg.
fb_org_useful_points: double dex; setting leg circles out-to-in twice; support leg takes the final clipper
proposed_about_text: A double-dexterity compound off a clipper set: the setting leg circles the bag outside-to-inside twice before the recatch, with the support leg taking the final clipper delay.
proposed_movement_intuition: From a clipper set, bring the setting leg around the bag outside-to-inside twice, then hop off the support leg and bring the bag onto a clipper delay with it.

current_description: Standalone trick distinct from the barraging modifier.
fb_org_description: A double-dexterity mirage; plant the setting leg, bring the support leg over in-to-out then over again, land on a toe delay with the original setting foot.
fb_org_useful_points: double-dex mirage; support leg crosses in-to-out twice; lands toe on the setting foot
proposed_about_text: A double-dexterity mirage: the support leg crosses the bag inside-to-outside twice before the toe recatch. Settable from clipper or toe, finishing on the original setting foot.
proposed_movement_intuition: Plant the setting leg and bring the support leg over the bag inside-to-outside, then over again, landing the bag on a toe delay with the original setting foot.

fb_org_description: A mirage into an osis; from a clipper, plant the setting leg and bring the other leg over (mirage) while hopping and spinning to land on an osis.
fb_org_useful_points: mirage flowing into an osis; plant + spin simultaneously
proposed_about_text: A compound that runs a mirage motion directly into an osis. From a clipper, the setting leg plants while the support leg crosses the bag, and a spin carries the recatch into an osis. Anchors the torque family.
proposed_movement_intuition: From a clipper, plant the setting leg and bring the other leg over the bag as in a mirage, then hop off and spin in the same direction to land on an osis with the original foot.

fb_org_description: From a clipper set, turn so the bag passes behind your back, then without replanting the setting foot spin into a mirage and catch on an osis (opposite foot).
fb_org_useful_points: clipper set; bag passes behind back; no replant; spin into mirage; osis recatch
proposed_about_text: A spinning compound built on the torque, adding a gyro rotation so the bag travels behind the back before the recatch; the setting foot does not replant between the spin and the mirage.
proposed_movement_intuition: From a clipper set, turn so the bag passes behind your back, then without replanting the setting foot, spin into a mirage and catch on an osis with the opposite foot.

fb_org_description: (fb.org row exists, ADD 2, description EMPTY)
fb_org_useful_points: no fb.org prose; user ruling: swing element, open terminal
proposed_about_text: A swinging set element in which the bag rides a smooth pendular motion of a straight support leg. Unlike pixie/fairy/surging set primitives, a pendulum is complete once the swing occurs and may terminate in a toe stall, a kick, a hand catch, or another trick. It is the reverse of the rake.
proposed_movement_intuition: Keep the leg straight and accelerate smoothly through the swing/flick, letting the bag ride the motion; the terminal is open (stall, kick, catch, or a follow-on trick).

fb_org_description: (fb.org row exists, ADD 2, description EMPTY)
fb_org_useful_points: no fb.org prose; memory: rake = scoop->toe-stall, reverse of pendulum
proposed_about_text: A swing-element dexterity that scoops the bag through a pendular motion into a toe stall; it is the directional reverse of the pendulum. Rake and pendulum together form the swing-element pair.
proposed_movement_intuition: Scoop the bag through a smooth swing into a toe stall, the mirror of the pendulum swing.

current_description: Jump and kick the bag with the toes with the kicking leg tucked behind (fborg-2add).
fb_org_description: (fb.org row exists, ADD 2, description EMPTY)
fb_org_useful_points: no fb.org prose; user ruling: like flying-clipper but cross-body toe contact
proposed_about_text: A jumping kick that contacts the bag with the toes while the kicking leg is tucked behind the support leg, cross-body. It is the toe-contact counterpart of the flying clipper: same cross-body tuck, but the toe surface meets the bag instead of the inside (clipper) surface.
proposed_movement_intuition: Jump and tuck the kicking leg behind the support leg, meeting the bag with the toes across your body rather than the inside of the foot.

current_description: Miraging kick: the opposite-side inside miraging dex without a terminal stall.
fb_org_description: (no fb.org match)
fb_org_useful_points: user ruling: kick-only miraging dex atom; relates to mirage
recommendation: RELATE-to-mirage (description adequate; surface the mirage relation)
proposed_about_text: The kick-only form of the miraging inside dexterity: an opposite-side inside dex performed without a terminal stall. It is the kicked dex atom of the mirage family, not an isolated concept.
proposed_movement_intuition: Perform the inside miraging dex and strike the bag without catching it, the no-stall kick version of a mirage entry.

fb_org_description: (no fb.org prose match)
fb_org_useful_points: user ruling: inside kick, kicking leg tucked behind support leg
proposed_about_text: A jumping inside kick with the kicking leg tucked behind the support leg, cross-body. It is the inside-surface counterpart of the toe-clipper.
proposed_movement_intuition: Jump and tuck the kicking leg behind the support leg, striking the bag with the inside of the foot across your body without catching it.

fb_org_description: (fb.org row exists, ADD 1, description EMPTY)
fb_org_useful_points: no fb.org prose; ladder spin->double-spin->triple-spin->spinning
proposed_about_text: A single 360-degree body rotation performed between contacts. It is the first rung of the spin ladder (spin, double-spin, triple-spin) and the trick form of the spinning modifier.
proposed_movement_intuition: Rotate the body a full turn between kicks, keeping the bag controlled before and after the spin.

current_description: Double kick: jump and contact the bag with both feet.
fb_org_description: (no fb.org match)
fb_org_useful_points: already complete; sibling of double-knee (parity)
proposed_about_text: A jumping move that contacts the bag with both feet in the air; the foot analogue of the double knee.
proposed_movement_intuition: Jump and strike the bag with both feet in quick succession before landing.
